title: 第五日剛出廁所
body: //主角從廁所出來兩人一起司徒基地 Frances Cheng: Are you ready? Frances Cheng: I am. Let's go. Frances Cheng: Or, is there something you want to say? <> <> <> //if知道23的事情 <<#If($TwentyTwoTruth=0||$TwentyTwoTruth=1)>> <> <<#EndIf>> //if知道隆生要亂搞的事情 <<#If($RonEnding>2.1)>> <> <<#EndIf>> <<@回答芳隆生或23問題>> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Nothing. Let's go. Frances Cheng: Sure! ->Why ask? ($DLStateFloat);//選過消失 <> Frances Cheng: Because I fell asleep before you came back last night. Frances Cheng: Did I miss anything? <<#GoTo(@回答芳隆生或23問題)>> ->23 has been hindering Situ's operation. ($DLStateFloat2);//if昨天有23真相，才出現 <<@對程芳說明23背叛真相>> <> <> Frances Cheng: Wh-what...!? Frances Cheng: But why? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Because this whole operation is one giant adversity test for Situ. Frances Cheng: Adversity test? Frances Cheng: Ah, she did explain that to me... ->Because Grandma made a trap for Situ. Frances Cheng: Isn't Grandma supposed to be her boss? Why would she do that? Frances Cheng: ... Frances Cheng: Don't tell me this whole operation is one giant adversity test? <<#Simple(8)>> ->I guess so. // ->How did you know about adversity test? Frances Cheng: Nin told me about it once. ->... <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: So... 23 doesn't want Nin to take this test? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->About that I'm not quite sure. ->Pretty much. <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: ... Frances Cheng: She's trying to protect Nin, huh... Frances Cheng: That explains a lot of things... Frances Cheng: But how did you know? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->She tried to knock Situ out with tranquilizer last night. Frances Cheng: What— ->She was kinda weird last night. Frances Cheng: What happened then? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Then I walked in on her when she tried to knock Situ out. ->Then she and Situ had this fight in front of me. <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: ... Frances Cheng: I presume, then, that 23 won't be able to join us today? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Yeah. ->Situ said she'd take good care of her. <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: ... Frances Cheng: What about Lawrence? Does he know? <<#If($TwentyTwoTruth=0)>> <> <> <<#EndIf>> <<#If($TwentyTwoTruth=1)>> <> <> <<#EndIf>> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->He will be informed, or so Situ said. ($DLStateFloat3);//if說要告訴他。 ->He will know. ($DLStateFloat3);//if說要告訴他。 Frances Cheng: I'm surprised. I thought Nin would rather keep him in the dark. <<#Simple(8)>> ->She would, but I talked her out of it. Frances Cheng: I see. ->Do you think he should be kept in the dark? Frances Cheng: ...I don't know. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: I would do that, if I were Nin, probably. Frances Cheng: But is that the right thing to do? I really have no idea. ->Situ said we should keep the truth from him, for now. ($DLStateFloat4);//if說別告訴他。 ->He doesn't. ($DLStateFloat4);//if說別告訴他。 Frances Cheng: ...I can't imagine how Lawrence would process the information. Frances Cheng: Poor boy. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Poor boy? Why? Frances Cheng: Being betrayed and distrusted by the people he looks up to. Frances Cheng: He will be devastated. ->Do you think we should tell him everything? Frances Cheng: I would rather not tell him, if it's up to me, probably. Frances Cheng: But is that the right thing to do? I really have no idea. <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: ... Frances Cheng: Alright. Frances Cheng: That settles that. Frances Cheng: Did anything else happen last night? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Nothing. Frances Cheng: Good. Let's go, then. ->Lawrence wants to publish everything we have on Vision, without Situ's consent. ($DLStateFloat5);//if昨天有知道隆生計畫，才出現，之前選過消失 <<#GoTo(@對程芳隆生胡來計畫)>> <<#EndSimple>> ->Lawrence wants to publish everything we have on Vision, without Situ's consent. ($DLStateFloat5);//if昨天有知道隆生計畫，才出現 <<@對程芳隆生胡來計畫>> <> Frances Cheng: ... Frances Cheng: *Sigh* Frances Cheng: ...Can't say I'm surprised. It's a very Lawrence decision. Frances Cheng: Did you tell Nin? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Not yet. Frances Cheng: Not yet. Frances Cheng: Meaning you plan to tell her? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->No. Frances Cheng: But you should. <<#Simple(6)>> ->Why? ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->Yes. Frances Cheng: Good call. <<#EndSimple>> ->I'm not planning to do that. Frances Cheng: ... <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: The situation could turn very ugly if you keep it from Nin. Frances Cheng: Lawrence doesn't know what he's doing. He's not thinking it through. Frances Cheng: At this rage, he and Nin could trip over each other. Frances Cheng: We must let Nin know about Lawrence's plan. Frances Cheng: ... Frances Cheng: I guess he believes he's upholding justice. Frances Cheng: But he has no idea how many people he will hurt as collateral damage... <<#Simple(6)>> ->You don't agree with him? Frances Cheng: Right. Frances Cheng: Didn't we cover this subject yesterday? Frances Cheng: Disclosing what we have on Vision to the world could have unpredictable consequences. Too risky. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng: Is there anything else that I should know? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Nothing. Let's go. Frances Cheng: Yeah. ->23 has been hindering Situ's operation. ($DLStateFloat2);//if昨天有23真相，才出現，之前選過消失 <<#GoTo(@對程芳說明23背叛真相)>> <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndSimple>> <<#End>>

title: 司徒基地討論進攻計畫
body: Nin Situ: Everyone is here. Good. Lawrence Chang: Eh...? Lawrence Chang: Not everyone. Where's 23? //if玩家不知道23真相or沒說服司徒告訴隆生真相 <<#If($TwentyTwoTruth=0||$TwentyTwoTruth=-1)>> Nin Situ: About that. Nin Situ: She's occupied today. OWL called her back. Emergency mission. Lawrence Chang: But what about our mission today...? Nin Situ: She left us all the hardware and software we need. The mission goes on as planned. //if玩家真的不知情 <<#If($TwentyTwoTruth=-1)>> Thought: Fran's looking at me, inquiring. <<#Simple(8)>> ->What about dispatch and intel? Nin Situ: 23 gave me access to all systems. That will not be a problem. ->Can't she make it back in time? Nin Situ: ...Yes. Thought: Situ's slightly hesitating...? ->... <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndIf>> Nin Situ: Frances, I need you to take over 23's position. Nin Situ: If everything goes as planned, you won't need to do a thing. Nin Situ: Still, there should be someone managing comms, just in case. Frances Cheng: ...Leave it to me. Nin Situ: Any objection, Lawrence? Lawrence Chang: Ah... Lawrence Chang: No, I'm all good, I think. //if芳知道隆生的事情 <<#If($FrancesKnow23Truth=0)>> Thought: Fran seems concerned. <<#EndIf>> <<#EndIf>> //if司徒說會告訴隆生23真相 <<#If($TwentyTwoTruth=1)>> Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: ... Lawrence Chang: ...Wh-what? Nin Situ: 23 won't be with us today. Nin Situ: Because she is the snitch among us. Lawrence Chang: What the— //if廁所出來沒跟呈芳說23 <<#If($FrancesKnow23Truth=-1)>> Frances Cheng: No way!? <<#EndIf>> Nin Situ: She has been tipping Vision off with our plans. Lawrence Chang: That's impossible! Lawrence Chang: 23 has no reason to— Nin Situ: She does. She believes this whole operation against Vision is an adversity test of Grandma's, specifically designed for me. Nin Situ: ...So she wants me to circumvent it. //if廁所出來沒跟呈芳說23 <<#If($FrancesKnow23Truth=-1)>> Frances Cheng: Adversity test, like you said... Nin Situ: Indeed, challenges created by Grandma to find out the limit and nature of OWL agents. <<#EndIf>> Lawrence Chang: ... Lawrence Chang: For real...? Nin Situ: She tried to knock me out with tranquilizer. Jason saw it. Lawrence Chang: Did you, Jason?;//隆生轉向主角 //if廁所出來沒跟呈芳說23 <<#If($FrancesKnow23Truth=-1)>> Frances Cheng: You knew about it, J!? <<#EndIf>> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Exactly as she said. ->She just wants to keep Situ from today's mission. <<#EndSimple>> Lawrence Chang: ... Lawrence Chang: No way... Lawrence Chang: 23 betrayed us...? Nin Situ: No. She didn't betray us. Whatever she did, it was to protect me. Lawrence Chang: Protect? By sabotaging the operation and exposing us to danger!? Lawrence Chang: I trusted her! Lawrence Chang: We all did!!! Lawrence Chang: And she almost had me and Jason killed!? Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: I didn't want you to know, Lawrence. Nin Situ: At least, not today. Lawrence Chang: Why...? Lawrence Chang: ... Lawrence Chang: Why would you do that? I have the right to know! Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: Lawrence, listen. Nin Situ: I kept things from you, because I didn't trust you. Lawrence Chang: Finally... Lawrence Chang: Now you're admitting it... Lawrence Chang: I've been waiting for this moment for a long time. Lawrence Chang: So it's not just me being paranoid. Lawrence Chang: No matter what I do, how hard I try, you just never, ever, trust me enough. Lawrence Chang: Fuck me. Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: I don't trust you to process certain information in a mature way, Lawrence. Nin Situ: Sometimes knowing too much makes you act on impulse, feeling unnecessarily insecure or even betrayed. Nin Situ: Right now is one good example. Lawrence Chang: ... Lawrence Chang: ...Then why did you tell me about 23? Nin Situ: Because Jason convinced me. Nin Situ: He made me think. Maybe I'm not treating you fairly enough. Maybe you deserve more trust. Lawrence Chang: ... Nin Situ: What I think is good for you may not be good or fair. Nin Situ: I thought I was doing it for your good, but in fact I was just trying to save myself some trouble. Lawrence Chang: ... Nin Situ: Conclusion: I was wrong. I should have trusted you more, or else you will never grow up. Nin Situ: I should have realized that a long time ago. Lawrence Chang: ... Nin Situ: That's it, Lawrence. I won't make any additional comment on this subject. Nin Situ: How to face this reality is entirely up to you. Nin Situ: Will you go berserk as I once expected? Nin Situ: Or will you swallow the pain and think it through? Nin Situ: Face it like an adult? Lawrence Chang: ... Lawrence Chang: ... Lawrence Chang: ... Lawrence Chang: Despite the situation of 23, our top priority is to stop the torture in the facility and save as many as we can. Nin Situ: Technically, we do not know if there is any torture going on yet. Nin Situ: Still, we must infiltrate the facility to find out. This cannot wait. Lawrence Chang: Exactly. 23's absence has no impact on that. Lawrence Chang: I can throw a tantrum right now. Hell, I'm tempted to... Lawrence Chang: But that's not productive at all, right? Nin? Lawrence Chang: So, let's talk about today's plan instead. Thought: Situ approves. Nin Situ: Indeed. Nin Situ: Let's begin. Lawrence Chang: As to 23... Lawrence Chang: Let's have a separate talk about that when this is over. Lawrence Chang: Shall we, Nin? Nin Situ: Of course. Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: You do deserve more trust, Lawrence. Thought: Situ is visibly relieved. Lawrence Chang: ... Lawrence Chang: Of course I do! Thought: Fran is giving me a thumbs-up. Thought: She's pretty happy, too. <<#EndIf>> Nin Situ: Now, let's move on to today's mission. Nin Situ: First, the objective. Nin Situ: We must infiltrate the facility in the shortest time possible, and find as much information as we can. Nin Situ: For that, I will rely on you, Lawrence and Jason. Nin Situ: Data acquisition takes time. During the process we may be detected or intercepted. Nin Situ: Here is the breakdown of responsibilities. Nin Situ: Lawrence and Jason, you two are in charge of data acquisition. Cover each other's back while you're at it. Nin Situ: I will keep watch and handle any threat. If you attract too much attention, I'll be the decoy. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Meaning we don't need to worry about combat? Nin Situ: Ideally, yes. Nin Situ: But you know as much as I do that the ideal situation rarely happens. Be prepared. ->With 23's decryption device like before? Nin Situ: Yes. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: Now, the infiltration plan. Nin Situ: Starting from the breakthrough point and route. //投影出現拉遠的李腦工廠空間投影 Nin Situ: Here is the floor plan of the facility, recovered by 23. Nin Situ: The facility itself is situated beneath Silencio. Nin Situ: The floor plan doesn't say much about what each zone is for, but the structure and security measures are clear enough. Nin Situ: As to our target... Nin Situ: It should be the server room, by common sense. //放大伺服器機房位置(很深的地下) Nin Situ: However, as we can see, it's located very deep down, and heavily guarded. Nin Situ: That makes it an impractical target. Nin Situ: Instead, we go for the next best thing. //放大實驗空間 Nin Situ: The main lab, which is a lot closer to the surface. Nin Situ: Be aware that our purpose is to find out what this facility is for. In that sense, we don't have to get all the data. Nin Situ: I believe the main lab should have everything we need. Nin Situ: Now, pay close attention. I will go over the exact plan, step by step. Nin Situ: As you can see, the main lab is very close to the main entrance of the facility, which is good news.;//投影顯示月台跟實驗室 Nin Situ: My plan is to access it through the main entrance itself.;//出現一個箭頭從月台延長伸進實驗室 <<#Simple(8)>> ->Hold on. I thought you said "infiltration"? Nin Situ: I did. ->By "access", you mean? Nin Situ: Take down anyone in our way and break in. I will take care of that. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: If we do it quick enough, it's still considered infiltration. Nin Situ: I will take down all guards and disable security cameras before they can sound the alarm. Nin Situ: Next, you disable the alarm system, Lawrence, in case we miss anything. Lawrence Chang: Roger that. Nin Situ: Meanwhile, Jason, you will find a way to enter the lab. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Why me? Nin Situ: Because you're not familiar with alarm systems like Lawrence is, and we really don't have anyone else on the team. ->Can't I just kick the door down? Nin Situ: Afraid not. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: Based on this floor plan, the lab's entrance is guarded by two gates.;//放大實驗室那邊的模型，兩層門閃爍一下 Nin Situ: It doesn't say what kind of gates they are. Nin Situ: Based on experience, though, the inner gate should be something of light weight. People go in and out of the lab all the time, after all. Nin Situ: Probably just glass panels. Easy enough to deal with. Nin Situ: But the outer gate should be much sturdier. "Kicking it down" is unlikely. Nin Situ: However, there must be a lock of sorts on the outside. Nin Situ: Maybe security pass or iris scanner. Nin Situ: Finding the key to this lock is your job, Jason. Nin Situ: Look for anything that resembles a security pass, or pick up a researcher that I happen to knock out for biometric verification. Nin Situ: Any questions? Lawrence Chang: So far so good. <> <> <<@問怎麼進實驗室流程>> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Where am I supposed to find security passes? ($DLStateFloat);//選過消失 <> Nin Situ: Check the bodies on the ground. There will be plenty when I get inside. Nin Situ: See if they're carrying any. <<#GoTo(@問怎麼進實驗室流程)>> ->Iris scanner? ($DLStateFloat2);//選過消失 <> Nin Situ: The device that knows who you are by scanning your eyeballs. Nin Situ: If it's that kind of lock, hold one of the knocked out guards up to it and pry their eyes open. <<#GoTo(@問怎麼進實驗室流程)>> ->What if there's no visible lock? Nin Situ: That could happen, yes. ->I see. <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: Just in case, I have a Plan B. Nin Situ: I'll bring along some C4 bricks. Nin Situ: That should take care of most kinds of reinforced gates. Nin Situ: However, using C4 is essentially announcing our arrival to everyone in the facility. Lawrence Chang: ...Let's keep it as a last resort. Nin Situ: Correct. Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: If nothing goes wrong, the whole process should take about 10 minutes. Lawrence Chang: What about the lab staff? It can't be empty, right? Nin Situ: We could use a researcher or two, for quick answers. Lawrence Chang: I see. Nin Situ: Once inside the lab, you and Jason plug the decryption device in, for data acquisition. Nin Situ: That's basically the plan. Nin Situ: Any questions? <> <> <> <<@問司徒入侵計畫相關問題>> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->The plan sounds a bit...ruthless? ($DLStateFloat);//選過消失 <> Lawrence Chang: ...I agree with you, man. Lawrence Chang: Basically, kick the door down, knock out everything that moves, grab the data and get the hell out? Nin Situ: That's one way of putting it. Nin Situ: Being ruthless is the intended effect. Nin Situ: Don't forget we must negotiate with Vision afterwards. Nin Situ: For negotiation, we must show them what we're capable of. Nin Situ: We must be direct and loud. Nin Situ: And leave a big pile of mess behind, so that Vision knows we're not bluffing. Nin Situ: Otherwise if we just approach them, saying we have their secret data, they wouldn't believe it so easily. Nin Situ: Meanwhile, we are essentially making a statement. Nin Situ: That they can't keep any secret from us. We take it by force if necessary. Nin Situ: Also, speed is key in today's mission. Nin Situ: Since staying undetected is impossible, we should make it a quick job. Hit and run before they know what's happening. Lawrence Chang: That makes sense. <<#GoTo(@問司徒入侵計畫相關問題)>> ->What kind of answers do we want from the lab staff? ($DLStateFloat2);//選過消失 <> Nin Situ: Since it's a research facility, the lab staff must know what's going on inside. Nin Situ: While you acquire data from their computers, I will take a more direct approach. Nin Situ: Interrogate their people. Nin Situ: That way, we may have a grasp of the big picture before delving into the data pile. <<#GoTo(@問司徒入侵計畫相關問題)>> ->Could you please go over the process again? ($DLStateFloat3);//選過消失 <> Nin Situ: No problem. Nin Situ: We break in from the front entrance. Nin Situ: I will take care of all security personnel and cameras. Nin Situ: Lawrence shuts down the alarm system, while Jason finds a way to open the lab's outer gate. Nin Situ: Once that is done, we move on to the main lab. Nin Situ: You, Jason and Lawrence, acquire data with the decryption device. Get the data and pull out. <<#GoTo(@問司徒入侵計畫相關問題)>> ->I'm all clear. <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: Good. In that case... Lawrence Chang: ...How do we pull out? Escape route? Nin Situ: The same way that you went in. Thought: Speaking of, she hasn't mentioned how we're supposed to go to the facility... Nin Situ: Lawrence, Jason. You two take the Vision shuttle to the facility. Lawrence Chang: Um, isn't that, like, suicidal...? Nin Situ: The security system of Vision shuttles is weak. Nin Situ: We can hijack one shuttle and modify its destination without triggering any alarm. Nin Situ: 23 has identified the facility in Vision's shuttle station list. Nin Situ: It's called Sleeping God. Frances Cheng: Sleeping God? Nin Situ: Yes, the same name we saw in the Flop Core data. Frances Cheng: How will you enter the facility, Nin? Nin Situ: I have found a few infiltration paths, which unfortunately require very high mobility to utilize. Nin Situ: I will get in through one of these paths and neutralize security personnel for you. Nin Situ: As to the escape route... Nin Situ: 23 has it sorted out. The hijacked shuttle will be there waiting for us. Nin Situ: Jason and Lawrence, you two get out by shuttle as soon as we have the data. Nin Situ: I will cover your back, and make my own way out when it's time. Nin Situ: That is the plan. Nin Situ: In, out, not wasting a second. Lawrence Chang: Yes, ma'am. Nin Situ: This Sleeping God, whatever it is, means a lot to Vision. //if隆生知道23真相 <<#If($FrancesKnow23Truth=0||$FrancesKnow23Truth=1)>> Nin Situ: I will get to the bottom of it, even if Grandma intervenes in person. Nin Situ: I'm sure Vision has a dirty scheme to hide, and it's huge. <<#EndIf>> Nin Situ: Today is the day. We will end it, once and for all. Frances Cheng: ...I've been waiting to hear that the whole time. Frances Cheng: "We will end it, once and for all." Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: Lawrence, Jason. Here is the decryption device. //司徒寧丟東西給兩人接著 Nin Situ: And Lawrence, I need you to pick up some supplies for me. Lawrence Chang: Supplies? Nin Situ: From Ivan Zhang. 23 is supposed to pick up today. Nin Situ: ...;//用手機 Nin Situ: I'll send over two sets of coordinates, one for pick up, one for delivery. Lawrence Chang: You can leave that to me. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Why Lawrence? Nin Situ: Because he has a chopper to move bulky machinery. Nin Situ: It's a plasma cutter. I need that to break into the facility. Nin Situ: Better deploy it close to the point of entry in advance. ->Delivery? Nin Situ: Lawrence must move it to a specific destination after picking up. Nin Situ: It's a plasma cutter. I need that to break into the facility. Nin Situ: Better deploy it close to the point of entry in advance. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: I'll leave the rest to you. //隆生離開 <<#End>>

title: 捷運站跟隆生會合
body:
Lawrence Chang: Hey, Jason.
<<#Simple(8)>>
->Hey.
    //
->That was quick.
    Lawrence Chang: Well, it was okay.
->...
<<#EndSimple>>
Lawrence Chang: ...
Lawrence Chang: How do you feel? Nervous?
<<#Simple(99999)>>
->Nope.
    Lawrence Chang: For real?
    Lawrence Chang: I'm pretty nervous myself...
->A little bit.
    Lawrence Chang: Haha, me, too.
->Nervous? For what?
    Lawrence Chang: We're about to break into a heavily guarded secret facility, man!
<<#EndSimple>>

//if有讓司徒跟隆生說23的事情
<<#If($TwentyTwoTruth=1)>>
    <<#GoTo(@司徒有跟隆生說23的事情)>>
<<#EndIf>>

//if沒有第四日晚上跟隆生談心
<<#If($TalkToRonDayFour=-1||$TalkToRonDayFour=-2)>>
    <<#GoTo(@程芳出發提醒時間)>>
<<#EndIf>>

<<@司徒有跟隆生說23的事情>>
//if有跟隆生走道好結局
<<#If($RonEnding=1)>>
    Lawrence Chang: ...
    Lawrence Chang: I owe you a thank you.
    Lawrence Chang: Thanks to you, I have found the answer that I spent so much time looking for.
    <<#Simple(10)>>
    ->You mean our talk last night?
        Lawrence Chang: Yep.
    ->Don't thank me. You found the answer yourself.
        Lawrence Chang: I wouldn't if not for what you said.
    ->...
    <<#EndSimple>>
    //IF有23真相
    <<#If($TwentyTwoTruth=1)>>
        Lawrence Chang: And you convinced Nin to tell me about 23...
        Lawrence Chang: That means a lot to me. Really.
        Lawrence Chang: She'd totally keep me in the dark if not for you.
        Lawrence Chang: Now she is finally willing to trust me.
        Lawrence Chang: A large part of it is because of you. Haha...
        <<#Simple(8)>>
        ->I just gave her a little push.
        ->She did it because she always trusted you.
            Lawrence Chang: I know.
        ->...
        <<#EndSimple>>
    <<#EndIf>>
    Lawrence Chang: ...
    Lawrence Chang: Actually, I didn't sleep much last night. There was a lot to think about...
    Lawrence Chang: ...
    Lawrence Chang: But now I have it all figured out!
    Lawrence Chang: I mean, it's not like I'm suddenly not hating Vision or flop farming anymore...
    Lawrence Chang: The point is, I'm now finally seeing the big picture.
    Lawrence Chang: If I really want to do something for the world, a degree of detachment is necessary.
    Lawrence Chang: Between helping specific people and helping people in general, I must think for the long term.
    Lawrence Chang: In other words, think like Nin. Right?
    <<#Simple(99999)>>
    ->Probably.
        Lawrence Chang: Hey, what kind of response is that?
        Lawrence Chang: I forgive you, though, because I'm feeling great right now. Haha!
    ->Right.
        //
    ->You're better than her. I mean it.
        Lawrence Chang: Don't flatter me, haha...
    ->You're getting there.
        Lawrence Chang: ...Yeah!
    <<#EndSimple>>
    Thought: I can tell that he has found his direction.
    Thought: That's great.
    <<#GoTo(@程芳出發提醒時間)>>
<<#EndIf>>
    
    
//IF有23真相
<<#If($TwentyTwoTruth=1)>>
    Lawrence Chang: ...
    Lawrence Chang: I owe you a thank you.
    Lawrence Chang: If not for you, Nin would never tell me about 23.
    <<#Simple(8)>>
    ->I just gave her a little push.
    ->She did it because she always trusted you.
        Lawrence Chang: Right.
    ->...
    <<#EndSimple>>
    Lawrence Chang: Nin's trust means a lot to me.
    //IF要隆生藥背叛
    <<#If($RonEnding>2.1)>>
        Lawrence Chang: ...
        Lawrence Chang: Hey, Jason...
        Lawrence Chang: I—
        Lawrence Chang: I guess I should not screw with Nin's plan.
        <<#Simple(8)>>
        ->Why?
            //
        ->I see.
            Lawrence Chang: You're not even asking why, haha...
        ->...
        <<#EndSimple>>
        Lawrence Chang: I...
        Lawrence Chang: I just don't want to betray her trust.
        Lawrence Chang: It's not like I agree with her. But...
        Lawrence Chang: Since she chooses to trust me...
        Lawrence Chang: It's only fair that I have open discussion with her.
        Lawrence Chang: That, instead of screwing with her plan without telling her.
        <<Set($DLStateFloat,-1)>>
        <<#If($ToldSiTuRonBetray=0)>>
            <<Set($DLStateFloat,0)>>
        <<#EndIf>>
        <<#Simple(99999)>>
        ->A wise decision, Lawrence.
            Lawrence Chang: ...Yeah.
            Lawrence Chang: I'm glad that you agree!
        ->I don't think so. You don't have to give up your plan because of her.
            Lawrence Chang: ...Is that so?
        ->But I've told her about our plan. ($DLStateFloat);//if已經通報司徒
            Lawrence Chang: ...!?
            Lawrence Chang: Really...
            Lawrence Chang: ...Well, there's nothing I can do about it, then.
            //IF當初有說要支持隆生亂搞

title: 進到實驗室裡
body: Ray Fu: Hey, hold on!? Ray Fu: What the hell are you doing!? Ray Fu: Don't mess with it! Lawrence Chang: You could be killed, and all you can think of is work!?;<> Ray Fu: No! Of course not! Ray Fu: Trust me, you can't afford to screw that thing up! Nin Situ: Hold on, Jason and Lawrence. Lawrence Chang: But— Nin Situ: What is this facility for?;<> Nin Situ: Answer me. Ray Fu: ... Ray Fu: Don't tell me you have no idea what you're breaking into? Ray Fu: Ha! Haha...;<> Ray Fu: Really? You don't know what you're screwing with! Nin Situ: Jason, Lawrence. You can go on. Ray Fu: Wait!;<> Ray Fu: ... Ray Fu: As you can see, it's a research facility.;<> Ray Fu: And the research is about, hmm... Ray Fu: Flop generation with exponentially increased efficiency. Nin Situ: Does your research involve torturing civilians abducted from the Shithole? Ray Fu: The Shithole? Torture? The hell? Ray Fu: No such thing! I promise! Why would we abduct anybody?;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->But you did buy children from the Shithole. Ray Fu: That much is true, but we only extracted some of their chromosomes. Ray Fu: It's a non-invasive procedure. Certainly not torture!;<> Ray Fu: We need healthy samples. Torturing them defeats the purpose! Ray Fu: We're civilized people. There's nothing wrong with collecting genetic information. Ray Fu: It's just that using this information for commercial purposes isn't legal yet. Or else we'd totally do it in the open. Ray Fu: It's technically illegal, yes, but I swear we'd never hurt any child!;<> ->Am I supposed to trust you like that? Ray Fu: A swear is as much as I can give. It's not like I have any hard evidence to show! ->... <<#EndSimple>> Thought: I don't think he's lying. Thought: Does that mean Chameleon is lying? Thought: This place is a trap? Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: Your boss spent an incredible amount of resources to keep this facility a secret. Ray Fu: Of course, it's top secret!;<> Ray Fu: It's the Red Room's most prominent project! Ray Fu: If any word gets out, our competitors will pay billions to replicate or steal our work! Ray Fu: I have been living in the lab for the better part of a year! So has the whole team. That's how serious we are. Nin Situ: ... Ray Fu: I know a lot of things we do are not exactly kosher... Ray Fu: But the bottom line is, nothing is done against anyone's will, and no one was hurt in the process. Ray Fu: That much I swear.;<> Nin Situ: Then explain what you do, in details. Ray Fu: I already told you. We are working on next-gen technology of augmenting computers with the human brain. Nin Situ: ... Ray Fu: ...You don't look convinced. Nin Situ: Considering the scale and security measures of this facility, and how hard Vision tries to keep it a secret... Nin Situ: Of course I'm not convinced. Ray Fu: That's because you don't see the commercial potential of our work! Ray Fu: Once done, Vision will kill the competition. The world is its oyster! Ray Fu: Right, there's something you must have heard of... //IF雙贏+IF單贏篩選 <<#If($FabWinWin=0||$ConvincedLaiJie=0)>> Ray Fu: The board is considering repurposing some outdated farms into charity facilities. Ray Fu: Offer Shithole crawlers a place to stay and a job to do, to ease the unemployment issue for the government. Ray Fu: You know how they can afford to do that? Because of what we do in this lab! <<#GoTo(@跳過另一個寂靜未來可能性對話)>> <<#EndIf>> //IF單贏操作+IF單輸(不是) Ray Fu: The board is planning to shut down outdated farms these days. Ray Fu: But why? You must be wondering. These old junks don't make big money, but still turn a profit. Ray Fu: How can they afford to get rid of them? Well, because of what we do in this lab! <<@跳過另一個寂靜未來可能性對話>> Ray Fu: This is where the future of Vision begins! Ray Fu: We're—! Nin Situ: Enough. Nin Situ: I'm not here for a business pitch. Ray Fu: ... Nin Situ: What exactly is the technology you're working on? Ray Fu: About that... Nin Situ: Answer me, in details. Ray Fu: ... Ray Fu: I see.;<>;<> Ray Fu: Let me show you. <<#End>>

Seth Lo: He's gone, and you don't have access to the elevator, anyway. Seth Lo: All you can do is to get out of here while you still can, or... Don't tell me you don't have the guts to do it? Thought: What the hell is his problem? Seth Lo: I see. Seth Lo: You're not motivated enough. Seth Lo: Between us, you don't have a score to settle. Seth Lo: I didn't touch your sweetheart. You barely know me at all. Seth Lo: If I were Chameleon, you'd be happy to finish me off. <<#Simple(12)>> ->I don't get it. Are you asking me to kill you? Seth Lo: I don't care. Death doesn't scare me. ->Yeah, I kinda wish you were him. // ->[Kill him] <> <<#End>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Seth Lo: You know what? Chameleon's right. Seth Lo: He knows your kind all too well. Seth Lo: You won't kill me, only because you don't want to get your hands dirty. Seth Lo: Anyone with two brain cells to rub together could tell I'd find you and kill you if given a second chance. Seth Lo: Still, you won't kill me here and now. Seth Lo: Because choosing not to kill makes you feel good about yourself. Seth Lo: It makes you feel like a big hero with a big heart. Seth Lo: Stupid hypocrite. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->You're awfully cynical for a villain. Seth Lo: Can't help it. Living among idiots and hypocrites like you makes a cynic out of me. <<@跟副官嘴砲最後階段>> Seth Lo: You know I'm right. Just admit it. Seth Lo: You know you should kill me, but you're too good to do it. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Alright, you win. I should kill you here and now. Seth Lo: Do it. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I just won't. Come on, make me. Seth Lo: You motherf— <<#End>> ->[Kill him] <> <<#End>> <<#EndSimple>> ->A hypocrite at least tries to look good. You're not even trying. Which is worse? ->Who are you to tell me what I should do? Seth Lo: You motherf— <<#End>> <<#EndSimple>> ->Yeah yeah yeah, believe whatever you want. Seth Lo: I've said my piece. Now it's your turn. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I won't kill you. ->Get up and kill yourself. Seth Lo: ...Hmph. ->[Kill him] <> <<#End>> <<#EndSimple>> ->I'm not a hero, but you're a villain alright. Seth Lo: ... Seth Lo: Trash talking doesn't change facts. <<#GoTo(@跟副官嘴砲最後階段)>> ->[Kill him] <> <<#End>> <<#EndSimple>> <<#End>>

title: 隆生主角進電梯追嚴勝和
body:
//晉電梯以後，螢幕有很微弱的異常特效
Lawrence Chang: Phew.
Lawrence Chang: Hope we're not too late.
Lawrence Chang: ...
<<#Simple(8)>>
->Do we know which floor we're going to?
    Lawrence Chang: Doesn't matter. There's only one stop.
->This elevator is pretty fast.
    Lawrence Chang: Yeah. Can't wait to get out of this hellhole.
->...
<<#EndSimple>>
Lawrence Chang: ...The floorplan says this elevator goes all the way up to Silencio's rooftop.
Thought: Rooftop?
Lawrence Chang: They must have built this elevator specifically for Yan.
Lawrence Chang: He uses the rooftop helipad all the time, doesn't he?
Lawrence Chang: Come to think of it, this underground facility must be the reason why he visits Silencio so often.
Lawrence Chang: ...
Lawrence Chang: *Cough*
Thought: Say...
Thought: It's a miracle that Lawrence made it out of the ambush alive.
<<#Simple(8)>>
->How did you get out of the ambush?
->How did you get that security pass?
    Lawrence Chang: ...
    Lawrence Chang: The power was out for a little while when the gate closed, remember?
    Thought: Right, all lights went out.
    Lawrence Chang: I hid in a room nearby during the blackout, stayed out of their sight.
    Lawrence Chang: Then I took them out one or two at a time from the shadows.
    Lawrence Chang: Before they knew what's going on, I found a security pass on one of them opened up a gate.
    Lawrence Chang: Good for me, I spent a ton of time studying the floor plan.
->...
<<#EndSimple>>

//引爆器發出逼逼生，隆生拿出並看著引爆器
Lawrence Chang: ...
Lawrence Chang: Looks like Nin is out of their ambush, too.
<<#Simple(8)>>
->How could you tell?
    //
->She's unkillable.
    Lawrence Chang: ...Yeah.
->...
<<#EndSimple>>
Lawrence Chang: The indicators on the detonator are lighting up, meaning she's deploying new explosives.
Lawrence Chang: Now we have about 10 sites ready to blow. She must be running out of C4.
Lawrence Chang: If we press the button now, the whole facility blows up.
<<#Simple(8)>>
->Why don't you press it?
    Lawrence Chang: Because Nin is still inside!
    Lawrence Chang: We might be out of the facility, but it's not the time for detonation yet.
->We should wait for Situ to do the honors.
    Lawrence Chang: Or we do it in her stead, if she can't make it out alive.
    <<#Simple(8)>>
    ->Come on, she's unkillable.
        Lawrence Chang: Ha...
    ->Right.
    ->...
    <<#EndSimple>>
->...
<<#EndSimple>>
Lawrence Chang: ...
Lawrence Chang: Ugh...;//隆生突然摀住腹部
<<#Simple(8)>>
->What's up? Are you alright?
->Don't tell me you got shot!?
->...
<<#EndSimple>>
Lawrence Chang: No, I'm fine.
Lawrence Chang: The stab wound I got the other day just broke open.
Lawrence Chang: Not a big deal.
<<#Simple(8)>>
->What if Yan is protected by a private army?
    Lawrence Chang: I don't think so...
    Lawrence Chang: The rooftop is supposed to be safe. Plus, they don't have any man to spare.
    Lawrence Chang: Right now I guess they're still busy looking for us down there.
->Can you move?
    Lawrence Chang: Not a problem.
    Lawrence Chang: Just need a breather.
->...
<<#EndSimple>>
Lawrence Chang: Let me sit down for a while...
//一些奇怪的暈眩效果，跟異常有關的特效放大

Thought: What the...?
Thought: My awareness is failing...
<<#End>>

title: 電梯內心世界
body: //這裡要寫一段主角跟玩家的總結，還有提到自己的意識變得模糊。可能是因為剛剛跟副官的戰鬥造成的。接下來可能會消失一陣子。 //主角靠槍昏過去進入內心世界。整個世界正在被異常的能量侵蝕 Jason Dai: Hello again. Jason Dai: Looks like I did it. <<#Simple(8)>> ->What's going on? // ->You dragged me in? Jason Dai: Yes. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: Listen. Jason Dai: This place is changing, as you can tell. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->You mean this black stuff? // ->The specter. Jason Dai: The thing that you told me about? //if第一日提 <<#If($DayOneTellMCErrorKill=0)>> Jason Dai: The one that killed everyone and set you free. <<#EndIf>> //if第二日提 <<#If($DayTwoTellMCError=0)>> Jason Dai: The one that somehow crippled Situ's severance device. <<#EndIf>> Jason Dai: This is it? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Looks like it. ->I'm not really sure. <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: This stuff is eating into my awareness. I think I'm losing it. <<#Simple(8)>> ->How can I help you? //if壞關係 <<#If($RelationshipWithMC<-3)>> Jason Dai: Don't bother. Jason Dai: You know you don't give two shits about me. <<#EndIf>> Jason Dai: Plus, you can't do anything about it anyway. ->What do you mean? Jason Dai: It's hard to explain. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: Listen up. Jason Dai: Whatever happens next, chances are I won't be with you anymore. I'm evaporating. <<#Simple(8)>> ->You're dying? Jason Dai: No, not dying. Jason Dai: I will be gone for a while, not for good. ->Evaporating? Jason Dai: Trust me, I'm as confused as you are. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: Somehow, this stuff isn't affecting you. <<#Simple(8)>> ->But I can't help you. Jason Dai: You can, in a sense. ->Are you sure it's not affecting me? Jason Dai: You tell me. Jason Dai: By the look of it, I'd say you're not feeling a thing. ->... <<#EndSimple>> //突然glitch又暴漲一點 Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: We are running out of time. There's something I've got to say... <<#If($RelationshipWithMC<-3)>> Jason Dai: You probably don't want to listen, but I've got to say it anyway. <<#EndIf>> Jason Dai: You have the stun grenade on you, right? Jason Dai: Use it the moment things start to turn sour. Jason Dai: If everything fails, remember, we have Ivan Zhang and the Moonflowers to fall back on. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Yeah, I know. ->Why are we talking about this now? ->... <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: You will confront Shane Yan once the elevator stops. Jason Dai: That would be the end of everything. <<#Simple(6)>> ->So? Jason Dai: So. ->Can't wait for it. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: You will have to deal with him without me. Jason Dai: I, on the other hand, will be gone for an indefinite amount of time. Jason Dai: Or maybe... Jason Dai: Maybe I'll just be gone for good. //if關係不好 <<#If($RelationshipWithMC<-3)>> Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: Don't you dare fuck this up. Jason Dai: You don't have to heed my words, but think for your own interest. This is a critical moment. <<#Simple(8)>> ->I won't fuck it up. Jason Dai: You'd better. ->I have no problem against you. Jason Dai: As if I'd believe you. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: ...That's it, then. <<#Simple(6)>> ->You dragged me inside just to say that? Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: No. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: Being able to pull you in means I'm gaining control. Jason Dai: Sooner or later, I'll kick you out of my brain for good. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Are you gonna strangle me like Situ did to her alternative self? Jason Dai: Sure, if there's no other choice. ->Dude, you're barely keeping yourself together. Jason Dai: This is only temporary... ->... <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: I will be back. Jason Dai: And I'm taking back my body. Jason Dai: You...;//戴士傑虛弱 Jason Dai: You just wait... <<#End>> <<#EndIf>> //if關係可以 Jason Dai: Remember what we talked about yesterday? Jason Dai: About my choices. //if加入鴞隊但切割司徒or加入鴞隊但不切割司徒 <<#If($DayFourDecideFinalPath=0||$DayFourDecideFinalPath=1||$DayFourDecideFinalPath=2)>> Jason Dai: I knew Situ told you about her mentor. Jason Dai: About his gun, trust, blah blah. Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: None of that changes my mind. The overall situation remains unchanged. Jason Dai: I still believe we should join OWL and severe ties with Situ. Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: But then I saw those things in the facility... Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: I, uh... Jason Dai: Every time I think this world is thoroughly fucked up... Jason Dai: ...Somehow it manages to show me that things can be fuckeder upper... Jason Dai: Situ is right. These people have crossed the ultimate line. //if昨天說不切割司徒路線 <<#If($DayFourDecideFinalPath=1)>> Jason Dai: You're right, too. Jason Dai: I'm naïve. I have no idea how rotten this world could get. Jason Dai: If joining OWL means we must defend them... <<#EndIf>> //if昨天說逃跑路線 <<#If($DayFourDecideFinalPath=2)>> Jason Dai: Not only that... Jason Dai: We must defend them at the cost of Fran's future... Jason Dai: Well, fuck me... <<#EndIf>> <<@戴士傑跟玩家說不知選啥好了>> Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: I don't know anymore. I'm so, so lost. Overwhelmed. Jason Dai: Worse still, look at me, I'm evaporating. Jason Dai: Guess I'll have to leave the rest of it to you. Jason Dai: Whatever happens, steer my life for me. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I will. ->Your life is in good hands. Just come back as soon as you can. Jason Dai: Thank you. ->What if you come back only to find that I made the stupidest choice ever? Jason Dai: Can't blame you. Jason Dai: I know you have tried. <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: I wish we got to know each other under better circumstances... Jason Dai: Beggars can't be choosers. Right now I'm just glad that we got to know each other at all. <<#Simple(10)>> ->It's my honor. // ->Cheer up, dude. Jason Dai: Ha... ->I got you, brother. Jason Dai: I trust you with my life. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: See you later. Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: My friend. <<#End>>;//異常glitch吞噬內心世界，主角突然醒來 <<#EndIf>> //if復仇 <<#If($DayFourDecideFinalPath=3)>> Jason Dai: I know we don't exactly get along. Jason Dai: But after seeing those things in the facility... Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: I, uh... Jason Dai: Every time I think this world is thoroughly fucked up... Jason Dai: ...Somehow it manages to show me that things can be fuckeder upper... Jason Dai: And I guess OWL knows about it all along... Jason Dai: ... Jason Dai: I still don't agree with what you said yesterday. Jason Dai: I can't possibly say no to everyone who's morally imperfect and take on the whole world on my own. Jason Dai: The thing is... Jason Dai: What Vision has done is morally rotten. Jason Dai: They're insane. Jason Dai: Situ is right. These people have crossed the ultimate line. <<#GoTo(@戴士傑跟玩家說不知選啥好了)>> <<#EndIf>>

title: 離開電梯內心世界
body:
//主角醒來，隆生在關心

Lawrence Chang: Jason!? Are you alright?
<<#Simple(99999)>>
->I'm OK.
    Lawrence Chang: You were kinda zoning out for a second.
->Did I pass out or what?
    Lawrence Chang: You were kinda zoning out for a second.
<<#EndSimple>>
Lawrence Chang: Were you hurt, too?
Lawrence Chang: How bad it is?
<<#Simple(99999)>>
->No. Just a bit tired, is all.
    Lawrence Chang: ...Good.
->Nope, and I'm in better shape than you even if I were hurt.
    Lawrence Chang: Man, that is unnecessary...
<<#EndSimple>>
Lawrence Chang: ...
Lawrence Chang: Phew.
Lawrence Chang: It's not over yet. We've got to hang on.
//if隆生幹壞事
<<#If($RonEnding>2)>>
    //if主角支持
    <<#If($RonEnding=2.2)>>
        Lawrence Chang: Don't you worry.
        Lawrence Chang: I made a copy of everything.
        Lawrence Chang: If Nin changes her mind again, we can publish all this data.
    <<#EndIf>> 
    //if主角知道隆生亂搞但沒有支持
    <<#If($RonEnding=2.1||$RonEnding>2.2)>>
        Lawrence Chang: ...
        Lawrence Chang: At this stage, I don't mind telling you, Jason.
        Lawrence Chang: I made another copy of all the files we retrieved.
        <<#Simple(8)>>
        ->What?
            //
        ->Back in the lab?
            Lawrence Chang: Yes.
        ->...
        <<#EndSimple>>
        Lawrence Chang: If Nin changes her mind again, I will publish all this data.
        <<#Simple(8)>>
        ->She doesn't have a reason to backtrack again.
        ->She's gonna blow this place up. There's no going back now.
        ->...
        <<#EndSimple>>
        Lawrence Chang: Still, just in case...
        <<#Simple(8)>>
        ->They have no hostage. Situ won't let them go this time.
        ->Given the situation, the world will know what they have done one way or the other.
            Lawrence Chang: Unless we have a nasty surprise.
            Lawrence Chang: Like a certain someone backtracks again.
            Thought: ...
        ->...
        <<#EndSimple>>
    <<#EndIf>> 
<<#EndIf>>


//電梯傳來清新的音效「燈」
Lawrence Chang: The elevator's arriving.
<<#Simple(8)>>
->Are you ready to do this?
    Lawrence Chang: Yeah.
->I'm ready.
    Lawrence Chang: Me, too.
->...
<<#EndSimple>>
//電梯停下
<<Set($DLState,老闆電梯停下)>>
Lawrence Chang: ...
Lawrence Chang: Good.
Lawrence Chang: Let's do this.
<<#End>>

title: 頂樓跟嚴勝和對質
body: //嚴勝和轉頭揮手讓飛行器離開 Shane Yan: ... Shane Yan: What now? What do you people want? Lawrence Chang: Law enforcement will be here soon. You can't get away. Shane Yan: Law enforcement? Shane Yan: ...What the hell? Who are you working for?;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->We work for no one. Shane Yan: ...What? ->Just know that you're done for. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Lawrence Chang: We will tell the world what you're doing in this lab! Shane Yan: ...;<> Shane Yan: ...I see. Shane Yan: Hahahahaha!;<> Shane Yan: I've got it wrong the whole time. Shane Yan: Up to this moment, I've been wondering why you're attacking my farm and lab.;<> Shane Yan: Now I know. Shane Yan: There's no reason. You're just a bunch of idiots playing your hero game.;<> //隆生又開一槍 Lawrence Chang: Watch your mouth... Shane Yan: What for? Shane Yan: I was cautious because I thought you were working for someone.;<> Shane Yan: Better stay put until I figure out who it is. Shane Yan: Turns out there's nobody. Then you're nothing.;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I'm still happy to kill you for what you did to Fran. Shane Yan: Fran who? Shane Yan: Ah, Frances Cheng. Shane Yan: Then you're after the wrong guy.;<> Shane Yan: I never did a thing to Frances. Shane Yan: What happened to her is entirely Chameleon's fault. The idiot misunderstood me. Shane Yan: Whatever issue you have, settle it with him. Go your merry way.;<> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Lawrence Chang: Enough! Shut the hell up!;<> Lawrence Chang: Don't forget who has the gun. Shane Yan: Puh-lease. You'd have shot me long ago if you could. Lawrence Chang: ... Shane Yan: Shit keeps piling up. First that bitch Yuna Hsu and her little scheme.;<> Shane Yan: Then you clowns.;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->You think we don't have the guts to shoot you? Shane Yan: Think? No, I know it. <<@嚴勝和強調他不怕主角殺他>> Shane Yan: Ask your little pal there. He's all bravado, talking about law enforcement and whatnot.;<> Shane Yan: Come on, shoot me in the head and see how much information goes with me.;<> Shane Yan: How much evidence will I take with me to the grave, eh?;<> ->Shut your mouth before I shut it for you. Shane Yan: How? Shoot me in the face? I dare you. <<#GoTo(@嚴勝和強調他不怕主角殺他)>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Lawrence Chang: ...Brag as much as you want.;<>;<> Lawrence Chang: Your precious lab will be blown to smithereens in minutes. Shane Yan: WHAT!? Shane Yan: What have you done!?;<> Lawrence Chang: We deployed enough explosives to get the job done. Now all it takes is the press of one button. Shane Yan: Are you nuts!?;<> Shane Yan: Who do you think you are? Shane Yan: Vigilante? Superhero? Shane Yan: You stole corporate secrets, damaged substantial assets, disrupted our business and killed lawful employees.;<> Shane Yan: Which of these crimes would you call legal?;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->What about you? What you did in this lab is not legal, either. Shane Yan: You can't prove it's illegal.;<> Shane Yan: Are you licensed attorneys now? How much chance do you have if we bring this to a court of law? Shane Yan: Even if our operation is illegal, you can't fight crime with crime.;<> ->We have to. There's no way to stop you within legal boundaries. Shane Yan: Really well justified, huh. ->We did cross a few lines. Shane Yan: Exactly. ->Breaking laws doesn't necessarily mean we're the bad guys. Shane Yan: I'm glad we have an agreement. Shane Yan: But who decides who are the bad guys? You?;<> Shane Yan: What do you think we need laws for, huh?;<> <<#EndSimple>> Shane Yan: You're a bunch of lawless scoundrels dreaming of making the world a better place.;<> Shane Yan: If you really do want to, go find a proper job. Contribute to society with honest work. Lawrence Chang: Contribute, by working for your evil empire? I'm doing the world a huge favor by getting rid of Vision.;<> Shane Yan: Ha! Getting rid of Vision!;<> Shane Yan: Do you have any idea what happens if Vision really is gone? Shane Yan: Everyone relies on our services, be it individuals, corporations, or governments.;<> Shane Yan: Our flop farms and AI algorithms keep the country running in good order. Shane Yan: Suffice to say... Shane Yan: Vision is the foundation of our civilization. Shane Yan: Can you say no to any of that?;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->A toxic foundation, poisoning the civilization built on it. Shane Yan: Well, the people chose us.;<> ->Such arrogance. One corporation can't be the foundation of a whole civilization. Shane Yan: It's a fact.;<> Shane Yan: And it's not arrogance. I'm not saying we lead the civilization. We just provide the bedrock for it to stand on. ->You do have a point. Lawrence Chang: ... Shane Yan: I have a feeling that you're mocking me... Shane Yan: Otherwise you're smarter than I thought, Dai.;<> <<#EndSimple>> Shane Yan: We live in a democratic society, where the people choose what they want.;<> Shane Yan: Our corporation, our operation, is backed by the consent of the majority. Shane Yan: Think about what we stand for.;<> Shane Yan: Denouncing Vision essentially means denouncing our democracy, spitting in the eye of the people.;<> Shane Yan: What makes you think you're better than the majority?;<> Shane Yan: Goodwill? Higher moral ground? Shane Yan: Come on, see the world as it is.;<> Shane Yan: Pretty much everyone agrees that our society has a ton of problems.;<> Shane Yan: Did they do anything to fix it? No. "I'm just a nobody," they say, "can't do anything about it." Shane Yan: When it comes to taking credits, suddenly everyone is the big hero. Shane Yan: Hilarious.;<> Shane Yan: Do you know how much Vision has donated to charity this year alone? How many people have we helped?;<> Shane Yan: Donation earns us tax breaks alright. Still, count the number of actual people that we helped.;<> Shane Yan: Then ask yourself, how many have you helped?;<> Shane Yan: None. Zero. You were busy helping yourself, bargaining for a promotion or a raise. Shane Yan: Now you stand here accusing us of this and that. Why? Because your personal interest was touched. That's why.;<> Shane Yan: How much more do you want us to do, huh? We are not obliged to save the world. Shane Yan: All this talk about corporate social responsibility, about giving back to society...;<> Shane Yan: We did all that, and more, yet you're still unhappy. What exactly do you want from us?;<> Shane Yan: Vision must pay every single one of you lazy asses, huh? Is that it? Are you insane or are we stupid?;<> Shane Yan: We are fighting for survival, too. The corporate world is a shark tank, with all these competitors waiting to swallow us whole. Shane Yan: Sandwiched between competitors and policy makers, we're literally between a rock and a hard place. Shane Yan: Who's gonna help us, huh? Shane Yan: What you're doing today is fabricating allegations to blackmail me for more benefits.;<> Shane Yan: You don't know how the world works and don't want to find out. Shane Yan: It would be so convenient if we're the bad guys and you're the good guys. Shane Yan: Vision happens to fit the bill when you need an evil dragon. By slaying it, you complete your "heroic deeds".;<> Shane Yan: Deep down inside, you know it's not that simple. You know your heroic deeds has far-reaching consequences. Shane Yan: Our donations and technologies keep the world running. Without us, it's the end of the world.;<> Shane Yan: Ignorant hypocrites.;<>;//燈光開始稍微閃爍 Lawrence Chang: The world is going to shit exactly becaus—!! Shane Yan: Save it, boy.;<> Shane Yan: I don't need more than three brain cells to tell that you're blaming everything on us.;<> Shane Yan: Well, keep telling yourself that. It's a free world. Shane Yan: You know what I think?;<> Shane Yan: I think you're a bunch of losers, crybabies, forcing the winner to rig the game so that you don't look as bad. Shane Yan: Pathetic. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Maybe you do have a point, but that doesn't justify what you did.;<> ->An awfully long speech for an excuse. Aren't you a crybaby, too?;<> Shane Yan: ...What?;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->How do you justify what's happening in the lab?;<> ->You don't have an excuse to profit from the inhumane treatment of fellow humans.;<> Shane Yan: ... <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Dumping shit on everyone else doesn't make yourself any cleaner. ->People are not perfect, but that's no excuse for what you did. Shane Yan: ... <<#Simple(99999)>> ->No matter how rotten the world is, you can't add salt to the wound.;<> ->Vision has taken up the mantle of power, and with power comes responsibility. It's part of the deal.;<> Shane Yan: You... <<#Simple(99999)>> ->What else do you have to say, scum?;<> ->Enough. Listen to yourself. You're pathetic.;<> ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Shane Yan: I'm the—!!!;<> //燈光切掉，下一秒燈光打開，鴞隊都出現了在黑暗樁眼睛發著紅光。祖母也現身在嚴勝和旁邊。 <<#End>>

title: 鴞隊登場2
body: ?Grandma: Good day, you all.;<>;<>;<> Lawrence Chang: You're— <<#Simple(8)>> ->Good day. // ->You must be Grandma, I reckon? ?Grandma: They do call me that.;<> ->... <<#EndSimple>> ?Grandma: I'm the director of OWL. Lawrence Chang: Grandma... ?Grandma: Looks like Nin and 23 must have done the introduction for me. <<#Simple(8)>> ->They said a lot of nice things about you. ?Grandma: Oh, save the flattery. Lawrence Chang: ... ->... <<#EndSimple>> ?Grandma: Do not move. I advise you. ?Grandma: All agents here are authorized to kill on sight. Lawrence Chang: ...What's going on now? Lawrence Chang: Now that you're here, Grandma, you must know what Vision is doing. Lawrence Chang: Why aren't you arresting Shane Yan? Even if you work for them, there must be a bottom line somewhere? ?Grandma: Indeed. This bottom line is called the law.;<> ?Grandma: The research facility you just sabotaged is a lawful operation. Lawrence Chang: LAWFUL!? No way!;<> ?Grandma: Its operation was legalized just before lunch time today. <<#Simple(8)>> ->That means it's illegal for the bulk of its existence! ->What kind of sick legislation could legalize that? ->... <<#EndSimple>> ?Grandma: I don't feel like explaining. You may do the honors, Nin. //司徒寧從黑暗中走出 <<#End>>

title: 司徒寧頂樓登場
body: Lawrence Chang: Nin!?;<>;<>;<> Nin Situ: Lawrence, Jason... Lawrence Chang: What's going on!? Why are they—!?;<> Nin Situ: Calm down and listen.;<> Nin Situ: What I'm about to say is just as new and shocking to me.;<> Lawrence Chang: ...;<> Nin Situ: Vision's proprietary technology of organic computing based on engineered imitoids is now legal by way of protective legislation.;<> Lawrence Chang: Those test subjects we saw in the lab—!?;<> Nin Situ: Those are imitation humanoids, or "imitoids", now legally defined as non-human organic products. Nin Situ: The formal announcement is a few months away, but all law enforcement organizations have already been informed, OWL included.;<> Lawrence Chang: So they are here to...;<> ?Grandma: To protect lawful business from being sabotaged or terrorized. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Vision was doing human experiments before this new legislation. ?Grandma: Our legal system does not apply punishment retroactively.;<> ?Grandma: If we are aware of an illegal activity only after it's legalized, it's considered legal all along.;<> ->What about human trafficking? ?Grandma: That case is being investigated. Multiple Vision executives are named as suspects.;<> ?Grandma: As far as our evidence goes, Mr. Yan is not aware of their alleged activity.;<> ?Grandma: In that sense, he shall not be held responsible, unless new evidence surfaces. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Shane Yan attempted to murder my girlfriend and myself. That I do have evidence for. ?Grandma: Regular murder is not OWL's responsibility. Please contact the police. ->You'd better be serious. ?Grandma: We have evidence to support the claim. ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Lawrence Chang: The fuck...!?;<>;<> Lawrence Chang: You're letting Yan and Vision go, just like that??;<> Lawrence Chang: Go downstairs! Look at what these sick fucks have done!;<> Lawrence Chang: How the hell could they possibly get the green light!?;<> ?Grandma: The protective legislation procedure requires stringent confidentiality. Your question will not be answered.;<>;<> Lawrence Chang: Damn... Lawrence Chang: Nin! Aren't you saying something!?;<> ?Grandma: Indeed. Say something, Nin.;<> Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: Lawrence and Jason. Hand over the decryption device.;<> Lawrence Chang: Not you, too, Nin!?;<>;<> ?Grandma: ... ?Grandma: They are not very cooperative. Nin Situ: ...Please.;<>;<>;<> Lawrence Chang: ...;<>;<> //隆生根主角交出破解器。 <<#End>>

title: 第一次交完破解器後續
body: ?Grandma: My part is done. ?Grandma: Now it's your show, Nin. Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: Listen up, Lawrence and Jason.;<> Nin Situ: Grandma will escort you both to OWL's HQ, where you will stay until this case is officially closed. Nin Situ: This is necessary, in case you keep on harassing Vision.;<> Lawrence Chang: What!? They can't!;<> Nin Situ: They can't, but Grandma wants to, anyway.;<> Nin Situ: And I am not in a position to say no...;<> Lawrence Chang: ...;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->What if we swear total confidentiality? Nin Situ: Grandma will not be fooled. ->How long would that be? Nin Situ: As long as necessary, is all I could say. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: Be advised that such detentions tend to be very lengthy, sometimes indefinitely. Lawrence Chang: So what? I'm screwed?;<> Nin Situ: I have negotiated with Grandma. You are given one option.;<> Nin Situ: Join OWL. Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: Once initiated, there will be a training period, which may take a year or two.;<> Nin Situ: During this time, you will be under constant surveillance. Nin Situ: So will be all your family, friends, and anyone who walks too close to you. Nin Situ: ...;<> Nin Situ: I know this is far from ideal, but it's the best I could do. Sorry.;<> Lawrence Chang: ... Lawrence Chang: I can't believe it...;<>;<> Nin Situ: Lawrence, OWL is—;<> Lawrence Chang: OWL!?;<>;<>;<> Lawrence Chang: I can't believe you!;<> Lawrence Chang: You're okay with these sick fucks' experiments? Just like that?;<> Lawrence Chang: Didn't you say they're going too far?;<> Lawrence Chang: Now you're suddenly OK with it, because it's legalized?;<> Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: It's not that simple...;<>;<> Nin Situ: There's a hard reality I must deal with... Nin Situ: If we blow up or even temporarily disable this facility, the whole country will plummet into chaos.;<> Lawrence Chang: ...What do you mean?;<> Nin Situ: This facility has been providing the whole country with a gigantic amount of flops, distributed through Vision's farms. Nin Situ: The scale is far, far greater than we expected. Nin Situ: I case you didn't hear me clearly, these flops are distributed through all Vision farms. Nin Situ: This place alone supplies approximately 60% of all flops consumed nationwide.;<> Lawrence Chang: SIXTY percent!?;<>;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Impossible. The world would have known about it a long time ago. Nin Situ: ...The government worked very hard to keep it a secret.;<> Nin Situ: Project Sleeping God was created by jointly by the government and Vision Corporation.;<> ->You and 23 never suspected? Nin Situ: Did you ever suspect the sky is one glass dome adorned with cotton candy? Nin Situ: The idea is too insane to even think about...;<> Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: Plus, we're not supposed to have any suspect...;<> Nin Situ: ...Because the government worked very hard to keep it a secret. Nin Situ: Project Sleeping God was created by jointly by the government and Vision Corporation. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: Listen carefully...;<>;<> Nin Situ: I wouldn't care if disabling this facility only causes some business service outage.;<> Nin Situ: It's primary client is the national government itself, which channels flops into power grid, traffic regulation, and medical service systems...;<> Nin Situ: Now you know what I'm talking about.;<> Lawrence Chang: ...;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->So, let's pretend nothing happened. Nin Situ: ...We have to. ->Are you sure? They could be bullshitting. Nin Situ: They don't need to.;<> Nin Situ: Even if I insist, I can't stand against the entire OWL.;<> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Lawrence Chang: ... Lawrence Chang: Shit. //if隆生幹壞事但被勸停下或是有告訴司徒但沒告訴她 <<#If($RonEnding>2||$RonEnding=-2.5)>> Lawrence Chang: Just admit it. You want to chicken out.;<>;<>;<> Lawrence Chang: All your talk about doing the right thing. Now what?;<> Nin Situ: Conceding to reality is the right thing, Lawrence.;<> Nin Situ: I can't blow this place up, even if I manage to neutralize all OWL agents on site.;<>;<> Nin Situ: Because that will disrupt the life of millions. Nin Situ: Nationwide power outage. Paralyzed hospitals. Massive pileups on all highways... Lawrence Chang: AAARRRRGH!;<>;<>;<> Lawrence Chang: I've had enough! Lawrence Chang: I know about all that. Lawrence Chang: You can't just keep giving in!;<> Lawrence Chang: Don't you get it!?;<> Lawrence Chang: They aways! ALWAYS have excuses to hold you down! Lawrence Chang: Because they don't care about anything and you care about everything!;<> Lawrence Chang: Enough is enough! Lawrence Chang: You can't do a damn thing if you keep going after the perfect solution!;<> Nin Situ: ...;<> Lawrence Chang: Have you ever thought about it, Nin?;<> Lawrence Chang: Maybe you've been hiding from the truth all along.;<> Lawrence Chang: All your concerns are just excuses for your inaction. In fact— Nin Situ: No.;<>;<>;<> Nin Situ: My concerns are the driving force of my action.;<> Nin Situ: Putting them aside would be hiding from the truth. Nin Situ: Which is what you're doing right now.;<> Lawrence Chang: ...What? Nin Situ: I'm saying, if you want action for action's sake, to let off steam, go ahead and do it.;<> Nin Situ: You're entitled to do whatever you think is good for you.;<> Nin Situ: But listen up, Lawrence.;<> Nin Situ: Never, ever call it the right thing. Nin Situ: You're doing it for your own sake. Others won't necessarily agree with you.;<>;<> Lawrence Chang: I—;<> Nin Situ: You will not chalk your own desire for revenge up to justice.;<>;<> Lawrence Chang: ... Nin Situ: In a sense, you're right. Enough is enough.;<> Nin Situ: Time to choose your path, Lawrence. Nin Situ: Which one will you take?;<>;<> Lawrence Chang: ... Lawrence Chang: I—;<> Lawrence Chang: I'd rather— Lawrence Chang: ... Lawrence Chang: You know what I'd rather do!;<>;<> Lawrence Chang: I'm gonna fuck the consequences and tell the world everything. Nin Situ: ...;<> Lawrence Chang: You knew what I did behind you.;<> Lawrence Chang: Why didn't you tell Grandma? Why did you cover up for me? Nin Situ: We don't have time for this, Lawrence...;<> Lawrence Chang: I have to know!;<>;<>;<> Lawrence Chang: Why do you keep beating me down, yet back me up at the end of the day!?;<> Nin Situ: Because I care about you.;<>;<>;<> Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: I can't give you up.;<> Lawrence Chang: ...;<> Nin Situ: Now you must choose. We are out of time.;<> Lawrence Chang: ...;<> Lawrence Chang: I—;<> Lawrence Chang: I don't know. Nin Situ: Lawrence...;<> Lawrence Chang: You always know what you want and where you're going. I'm not like you.;<> Lawrence Chang: I'm utterly lost...;<> Lawrence Chang: ... Lawrence Chang: Even so... Lawrence Chang: I'm dead certain that something is very much wrong.;<> Lawrence Chang: Vision, OWL, these crazy scientists, and the fact that we must let them be...;<> Lawrence Chang: Everything is wrong.;<> Lawrence Chang: They're rewriting the definition of human, Nin!;<>;<> Nin Situ: I told you before, Lawrence.;<>;<> Nin Situ: To get anything done, first of all, you've got to have self control. Nin Situ: The control over your body and mind. Nin Situ: Including your sense of justice.;<> Lawrence Chang: Is justice still justice if it can be controlled?;<> Nin Situ: It's not, but you have to deal with it. Nin Situ: You won't achieve anything by confronting the whole world.;<> Nin Situ: To induce changes, first, change yourself.;<>;<> Lawrence Chang: ... Lawrence Chang: I always wanted to be like you, Nin...;<>;<> Lawrence Chang: Of course you know that.;<> Lawrence Chang: But now...;<> Lawrence Chang: I...;<>;<> <<#End>> //一名鴞隊開槍爆隆生的頭倒地。司徒寧也嚇到，主角下到看相隆生之類的？ <<#EndIf>> //if隆生沒幹壞事 Lawrence Chang: I don't know anymore. What have we been fighting for?;<> Nin Situ: Lawrence...;<> Lawrence Chang: Let me finish, please.;<>;<> Nin Situ: ...OK.;<> Lawrence Chang: You and Jason made me realize how I've been driven by blind rage.;<> Lawrence Chang: If I pursued revenge regardless of the cost, even sacrificing others... Lawrence Chang: ...That would make me the opposite of justice. Nin Situ: You don't have to choose justice over your own feelings.;<>;<> Lawrence Chang: I know.;<>;<> Lawrence Chang: But I want to. Nin Situ: ...;<> Lawrence Chang: I know there are consequences to consider, trade-offs to make.;<> Lawrence Chang: Sometimes, to move forward, I must back up a few steps first. Lawrence Chang: The question is... Where's the bottom line?;<> Lawrence Chang: If I must keep backing up indefinitely... Lawrence Chang: How am I supposed to go forward?;<> Nin Situ: Not indefinitely.;<>;<> Nin Situ: Every now and then we must concede with reality, but not always.;<> Nin Situ: I know how you feel. Sometimes it does look like the world is on a downward spiral, no matter what we do.;<> Nin Situ: It feels devastating, as if nothing we do has any meaning.;<> Nin Situ: But believe me, it's not true.;<> Nin Situ: Our job is not to save the world, Lawrence.;<> Nin Situ: The world will eventually tear itself apart. Our job is just to postpone that day. Nin Situ: By constantly removing rotten parts of society with precision. Nin Situ: The objective is just to buy the world more time. That's it.;<> Nin Situ: In this process, actual changes could happen, induced by people who are not us. Nin Situ: Starting from one spark, growing into a wildfire, fixing the downward spiral of self destruction.;<> Nin Situ: Before that happens, we just keep on fighting. Nin Situ: A lot of times we fail, but that doesn't mean our work is meaningless.;<> Nin Situ: ...;<> Nin Situ: Join OWL, Lawrence.;<>;<> Nin Situ: Join me. Together, we will buy the world more time.;<> Lawrence Chang: ...Isn't OWL one of the rotten parts?;<> Nin Situ: ...;<> Nin Situ: It doesn't have to be.;<> Lawrence Chang: ... Lawrence Chang: ...Can I just leave the decision to you, Nin?;<>;<> Nin Situ: No.;<> Nin Situ: This time, the choice and responsibility are entirely yours. Lawrence Chang: ...;<> Thought: ... Lawrence Chang: I have made up my mind, then. Lawrence Chang: I will join OWL.;<>;<> Nin Situ: ...Good.;<> ?Grandma: That settles that.;<> ?Grandma: Take the boy home.;<> Lawrence Chang: Nin... Nin Situ: Go with them. You will be fine.;<> Lawrence Chang: ...;<> Lawrence Chang: Hey, Jason...;<> Lawrence Chang: ... Lawrence Chang: See you later!;<> <<#Simple(10)>> ->See you later. ->... <<#EndSimple>> <> <<#Wait(3)>> ?Grandma: ...;<> ?Grandma: The boy has all your problems but none of your assets.;<> ?Grandma: He will break before the end of the first year. Nin Situ: ...You don't know him.;<> ?Grandma: But I know you. <<#End>>
