Nin Situ: Really? ->... <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: I was expecting complaints, about OWL, this situation...;<>;<> Nin Situ: And about me.;<>;<> <> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Yeah, you pushed me into this corner. Nin Situ: ...Yes.;<> Nin Situ: And for that I am sorry.;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I believed you. You said I could take my life back. Look where we are now. Nin Situ: I failed you. ->As if being sorry solves any problem. Nin Situ: ...You are right.;<> ->I know you're not one of the bad guys, but this is bullshit. <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: This is reality. <<#GoTo(@司徒說他無能為力現況如此)>> ->You know how sick the thing they do down there is? Nin Situ: I know.;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->And you choose to let it slide. Nin Situ: I have explained. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->But some lines are not supposed to be crossed! ->I don't accept your explanation. <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: This is reality. <<#GoTo(@司徒說他無能為力現況如此)>> ->You don't look like you care at all. Nin Situ: I do care, although I'm not able to do anything about it. <<#EndSimple>> <<@司徒說他無能為力現況如此>> Nin Situ: It's a dead end. I'm trying to choose the least of all evils.;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->You keep saying you can't do anything, but there is an option. Nin Situ: What would that be?;<> Nin Situ: Confront everything and everyone?;<> Nin Situ: Attack them in a suicidal charge and take as many of them as possible with me? Nin Situ: What does that change?;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->At least you'd make your stand. Nin Situ: No. Doing that would be discarding everything I've been fighting for.;<> Nin Situ: I don't need to make a stand with my death. I can do more for the world by staying alive.;<> ->You're a coward, Situ. Nin Situ: I'm not afraid of death, if that's what you mean.;<> Nin Situ: I'm afraid of meaningless death.;<> <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: I don't expect you to see the world my way. Nin Situ: We have differences, and I respect that. ->OK. I respect your effort. Nin Situ: ...Thank you.;<> <<#EndSimple>> ->I have nothing to say. Nin Situ: ...Good.;<> <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: ...;<> Nin Situ: ...;<> Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: Jason, about joining OWL...;<> Nin Situ: I know this is happening much sooner than we anticipated, but the situation is essentially the same.;<>;<> Nin Situ: Being what you are, you don't have a choice.;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I must yield. Nin Situ: If you call this yielding...;<> Nin Situ: ...Yes, you must.;<> ->Why do you bother asking? Nin Situ: ... ->I'm well aware. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: I know this can't look any worse. A life that you don't want is being forced upon you.;<>;<> Nin Situ: ...;<> Nin Situ: But please see it as an opportunity.;<> Nin Situ: You know this world is the oyster of a selected few with power and privilege.;<> Nin Situ: That is how they took away the old way of life from you and Frances. Nin Situ: Now you have a chance to change that.;<> Nin Situ: The world never changes, unless someone takes initiative and induce change.;<> Nin Situ: A lot of people choose to do nothing, just waiting to be swept away. Nin Situ: Now you have an opportunity to flip the table and take initiative.;<> Nin Situ: By joining OWL, you will have access to top secrets and make powerful friends.;<> Nin Situ: That makes you an influencer. You will have control over your own fate. Nin Situ: Moreover, you will influence the life of others, to shape the world toward you and Frances's dream.;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I've heard this speech before, when movie villains invite the hero to join their gang. Nin Situ: Interpret it that way if you want. Nin Situ: Joining OWL means gaining access to real power. How to use this power is up to you. ->From joining OWL to changing the world, there's a huge gap. Nin Situ: You can't change the world on your own. But it's a start. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Well, that's exactly my point. I won't change the world. ->A start, not necessarily with a proper ending. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: ... ->Look at yourself now. Where's this power when you need it? Nin Situ: ... ->... <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: OWL is a path, not the answer.;<> Nin Situ: A very convenient path, for most purposes.;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Yeah, pretty handy for governments and corporations. ->But this path has toll booths and I can't afford the fee. ->What am I then, one of the road pavers? Nin Situ: On a personal level, being an OWL agent means a lot.;<> Nin Situ: It opens gates to places that you can never dream of.;<> Nin Situ: You will be able to tackle problems proactively, instead of dealing with the aftermath.;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->There are restrictions, no? Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: Sure there are.;<> Nin Situ: Everything has a price.;<> Nin Situ: Is the price worth it? That is up to you.;<> Nin Situ: Imagine if you're a civilian, a nobody. Nin Situ: If you happen to be in the way of some greater power... Nin Situ: Then you're as good as dead. No fighting chance.;<> Nin Situ: By accepting the restrictions of OWL, everyone will think twice before touching you.;<> ->I can tackle problems proactively on my own. It's called being an adult. Nin Situ: I thought this case in front of us made a good enough point. Nin Situ: As a civilian adult, you had no choice or whatsoever, other than being tossed around. ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: ...Jason, I hate to force this onto you.;<> Nin Situ: You know as much as I do, that you have no other option.;<> Nin Situ: All I'm saying is that you may want to look at the bright side of life. Make the most out of what you have. Nin Situ: If you have any question, I will try my best to provide answers.;<> Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: It's the only thing I can do.;<> <> <> <> <> <> <<@加入鴞隊前跟司徒問答>> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->One way or the other, Shane Yan will get away with it, yes? ($DLStateFloat) <>;<> Nin Situ: ...Correct.;<> Nin Situ: For now. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I can't hold him responsible for the murder attempt on Fran? Nin Situ: ...;<> Nin Situ: I'm afraid so.;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Come on, not even some compensation? Nin Situ: ...That is unlikely. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->So what? We just swallow it? ->Can't you really do anything about it? Nin Situ: Right, and I'm sorry.;<> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: ... ->You will let him go? ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->How am I supposed to deal with that? Nin Situ: ...I don't know. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: Jason. Nin Situ: It's your life, your feelings. I don't have a say in it. Nin Situ: And I don't think anyone is entitled to tell you to accept it as-is. Nin Situ: ...;<> Nin Situ: But reality is reality. Nin Situ: Yan has an airtight legal team. We can't touch him.;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->What if this lab is blown up? Nin Situ: ...;<> Nin Situ: In that case, Vision will hold Yan responsible. There will be dire consequences. Nin Situ: But on the other hand, it will ruin the life of millions. Nin Situ: And if it's you who blows it up, you will be executed as a terrorist. Nin Situ: ...Of course, you won't be able to do it. This is all hypothetical.;<> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: Given the circumstances, what I'm about to say may not be very convincing...;<> Nin Situ: But I want you to think in the long term.;<> Nin Situ: As long as we keep up the fight, the bad guys will pay for what they did. ?Grandma: Watch it, Situ. Nin Situ: I mean it.;<> Nin Situ: Do you have any more questions?;<> <<#GoTo(@加入鴞隊前跟司徒問答)>> ->If I join OWL, will they keep Fran and me safe? ($DLStateFloat2) <>;<> Nin Situ: Definitely, if you mean your physical safety.;<> Nin Situ: OWL sees its agents as assets. Each of them takes substantial resources to train. Nin Situ: They won't allow their assets to be damaged, unless there is a good enough reason. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Good enough reason, huh. ->What if, for whatever reason, OWL wants to see me or Fran dead? Nin Situ: ...;<> Nin Situ: In that case, I will be your ally.;<> Nin Situ: But I can't promise you that the two of us could take on the entire OWL. That would be a blatant lie. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: OWL exists to safeguard the interests of leading corporations.;<> Nin Situ: One common interest they have is a country of law and order. Nin Situ: Stability is the key. Justice, transparency, trust, none of these matters. Nin Situ: I hope that answers your question.;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->It sort of does. Nin Situ: Do you have any other concerns? ->It doesn't. I need a firm promise. Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: In that case, I don't have one to give.;<> Nin Situ: Because I know nothing is for certain. Nin Situ: Still, given the situation, this is the best way out for you and Frances, or so I believe. Nin Situ: Do you have any other concerns?;<> <<#EndSimple>> <<#GoTo(@加入鴞隊前跟司徒問答)>> ->You just let these crazy scientists keep doing their thing? ($DLStateFloat3) <>;<> Nin Situ: ... Nin Situ: Yes, for the interest of the people.;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Being a member of OWL, you'd even have to protect them. ->Don't tell me OWL agents deal with shits like this all the time? Nin Situ: ...We do.;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->That's it? You just accept it as a normal part of life? Nin Situ: Not a normal part of life, but we have to accept it. ->Wow, fucking great. Nin Situ: ... ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: These corporations have no moral standard to speak of, but their products and services keep daily life running. Nin Situ: By bringing any of them down, you destroy a pillar of this country. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->If we leave them be, they'd keep exploiting everyone. Nin Situ: I can't argue with that. Nin Situ: But I'm not saying we must absolutely leave them alone.;<> Nin Situ: Sometimes we must teach them a lesson, but not now. There is too much at stake.;<> ->I can tell. I'm not stupid, you know. But do we just let them do whatever they want? Nin Situ: ...;<> Nin Situ: No, we must teach them a lesson every now and then. Nin Situ: But not now. There is too much at stake.;<> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: ...For your question, the answer is always there. You just refuse to see it. Nin Situ: Whatever we think or believe doesn't matter.;<> Nin Situ: In this particular case, we simply can't do anything about it. Nin Situ: Ask me something else.;<> <<#GoTo(@加入鴞隊前跟司徒問答)>> ->I have no questions. ($DLStateFloat4) <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: ...;<> <<#End>>

title: 收尾第一階段
body:
//等一下
Nin Situ: ...
Nin Situ: I will not tell lies just to make you feel more comfortable.;<<Play(司徒寧,MBU_司徒寧_機動輕裝_負傷低頭返回)>>
Nin Situ: Corporations like Vision are trampling on legal boundaries and moral standards.;<<Play(司徒寧,MBU_司徒寧_機動輕裝_負傷抬手)>>
Nin Situ: Despite their inhumane doing, there is no "evil" intent behind it.
Nin Situ: It's just that they do not see the consequences of their activities, and do not have to pay for any of it.;<<Play(司徒寧,MBU_司徒寧_機動輕裝_負傷抬手返回)>>
Nin Situ: As to OWL...;<<Play(司徒寧,MBU_司徒寧_機動輕裝_負傷隱忍)>>
Nin Situ: OWL is facilitating them.
Nin Situ: ...
Nin Situ: This reality has been apparent ever since the first day I joined.;<<Play(司徒寧,MBU_司徒寧_機動輕裝_負傷隱忍返回)>>
Nin Situ: Grandma knew what Vision was doing all along. She knows about my investigation, too.;<<Play(司徒寧,MBU_司徒寧_機動輕裝_負傷看內)>>
Nin Situ: However, she didn't mention any of it, watching me go after vision, until today.
Nin Situ: This is exactly what she intended.;<<Play(司徒寧,MBU_司徒寧_機動輕裝_負傷看內返回)>>
Nin Situ: ...;<<Play(司徒寧,MBU_司徒寧_機動輕裝_負傷隱忍)>>
Nin Situ: The purpose is to rile me up and wear me down...
Nin Situ: Till I have to admit defeat.
Nin Situ: It's a challenge to find out what it takes to break me, whether I have the guts to go rogue.;<<Play(司徒寧,MBU_司徒寧_機動輕裝_負傷隱忍返回)>>
Nin Situ: Am I wrong, Grandma?;<<Play(司徒寧,MBU_司徒寧_機動輕裝_負傷看內)>>
?Grandma: You are as smart as ever.
Nin Situ: ...;<<Play(司徒寧,MBU_司徒寧_機動輕裝_負傷看內返回)>>
Nin Situ: She wants to find out if I value the greater good over my own goals.
Nin Situ: Right now I can choose to blow this lab up and duke it out with OWL.;<<Play(司徒寧,MBU_司徒寧_機動輕裝_負傷低頭)>>
Nin Situ: That would achieve my own objective, which is to make our hard work count and make Vision pay for what they did.
Nin Situ: On the other hand...;<<Play(司徒寧,MBU_司徒寧_機動輕裝_負傷低頭返回)>>
Nin Situ: I can choose to tolerate Vision, for the sake of millions of people.
Nin Situ: You know what my choice is, Jason.;<<Play(司徒寧,MBU_司徒寧_機動輕裝_負傷說明抬手)>>
//IF有23案現
<<#If($TwentyTwoTruth=0||$TwentyTwoTruth=1)>>
    Nin Situ: I have kept my promise to 23, by staying faithful to my nature.
<<#EndIf>>
Nin Situ: I always know what I want, and what my priority is.;<<Set($DLState,司徒舉槍前拉近攝影機)>>
Nin Situ: The purpose of adversity tests is to force us to confront and forfeit our own values.
Nin Situ: In my case, constantly confronting myself is the very core of my values.;<<Play(司徒寧,MBU_司徒寧_機動輕裝_負傷說明抬手返回)>>
Nin Situ: I'm not giving in or giving up. My choice today is what I'd always choose, with or without the adversity test.;<<Play(司徒寧,MBU_司徒寧_機動輕裝_負傷低頭)>>
Nin Situ: I see in absolute clarity that this world is a filthy place.
Nin Situ: My death will not cleanse it. On the contrary, by making necessary trade-offs, I get to live and do more good.;<<Play(司徒寧,MBU_司徒寧_機動輕裝_負傷低頭返回)>>
Nin Situ: In other words, actively choosing to lose a battle, so that to not lose the war.;<<Play(司徒寧,MBU_司徒寧_機動輕裝_負傷前傾)>>
Nin Situ: As long as I keep fighting, wrongdoers will feel the pressure of a leash.
Nin Situ: Until one day, real change starts to happen.
Nin Situ: First a spark...;<<PlayUntil(司徒寧,MBU_司徒寧_機動輕裝_負傷前傾返回)>>
Nin Situ: Then, one day, a wildfire.;<<Set($DLState,司徒舉槍細微運鏡)>>;<<PlayUntil(司徒寧,MBU_司徒寧_機動輕裝_結局等待交槍)>>;//司徒寧拿出槍
Nin Situ: ...
Nin Situ: I have said enough.
Nin Situ: It's time, Jason.
Nin Situ: You have come a long way.;<<Play(戴士傑,MBU_戴士傑_便服3_頂樓對峙司徒冷靜轉側站隱忍)>>
Nin Situ: Now, make your choice.
<<Set($DLState,結局主角選擇攝影機)>>
<<#Wait(4.5)>>

title: 剛進實驗室
進到實驗室裡2
body:
Lawrence Chang: What the...!?
Thought: Are these...dead bodies?
Nin Situ: What is this...?
Ray Fu: Deceased prototypes of older iterations, demonstrating the progress of our research.
Nin Situ: ...Iterations of what?
Ray Fu: I told you.;<<Play(傅瑞光,MBU_傅瑞光_背站展示看左)>>;<<Set($DLState,不拍漂浮屍體了)>>
Ray Fu: We're working on a new technology of flop generation, with vastly improved efficiency.
Ray Fu: ...
Ray Fu: Instead of hiring juicers, we decided to grow our own brains in the lab. It's a lot cheaper.
Ray Fu: But soon we realized it's impossible to clone just a human brain...;<<Play(傅瑞光,MBU_傅瑞光_背站展示看左返回)>>
Ray Fu: Then we tried to do it the other way around. Clone a whole human, then start to do subtraction.
Nin Situ: ..Subtraction?
Ray Fu: Cutting unnecessary parts off, you know. We only need the brain. Most other organs are just a waste of nutrition.;<<Play(傅瑞光,MBU_傅瑞光_背站展示看左)>>
Ray Fu: This is where our lab excels, mind you. We're not your typical science nerds.
Ray Fu: We care a lot about engineering and real world application. Cost control is a big deal. We must simplify the design.
Ray Fu: One step at a time, we removed everything that is not necessary for flop generation.;<<Play(傅瑞光,MBU_傅瑞光_背站展示看左返回)>>
Ray Fu: Eventually we got this, a stable, efficient organic computing unit.;<<Set($DLState,中間展示開始升起)>>;<<Play(傅瑞光,MBU_傅瑞光_背站展示操作)>>;//中間平台開始升降
Lawrence Chang: Hold on. Is he saying what I think he's saying...?
<<#End>>

title: 進到實驗室裡3
body:
//肉人出現
Lawrence Chang: Fucking hell!
Thought: Is that...a human?
Ray Fu: Cost reduction comes first. A computing unit must be easy to maintain.
Ray Fu: By removing all limbs and some internal organs, its nutrition needs is exponentially reduced.
<<#End>>

title: 進到實驗室裡6
body: Ray Fu: There you go. Ray Fu: ...That's pretty much it.;<> Lawrence Chang: You're demons! Nin Situ: ...;<> Nin Situ: ...Is he still alive? Ray Fu: Of course, and acutely conscious, too. Or else it won't be able to generate any flops. We don't what that, right? Ray Fu: Oh, since it's grown in a lab with a massively sped up procedure, it doesn't have time to develop proper sentience.;<> Ray Fu: To get the brain working, we gave it a replicated awareness of a healthy human adult. Nin Situ: ...What does that mean? Ray Fu: We selected ideal, active juicers from Vision's farms, and copied, let's say, the memories of them. Ray Fu: Then we paste said memories, along with egos, into these computing units. Thought: ...Replicating memories? Is that possible? Lawrence Chang: Wait. That means...;<> Lawrence Chang: This thing genuinely believes it's a contributor racked up in a flop farm? Lawrence Chang: That makes it a real human! Thought: Inside this brain it could be a contributor in Silencio's Cellar...;<> Ray Fu: Allow me to correct you. First of all, this computing unit is not human. Lawrence Chang: NIN!;<> Lawrence Chang: We must help him. He's— Ray Fu: He?;<> Ray Fu: This is a computing unit, not a man. Ray Fu: It's an artificial creation based on human DNA and cutting-edge science. Ray Fu: You can't turn it back into a human, because it's never a human to begin with. Nin Situ: ...We can put it out of its misery.;<>;<> Lawrence Chang: Is there really no other way? <> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->There could be other ways to help him. Nin Situ: Don't be naïve. ->Let's just give him a quick death. Lawrence Chang: But—;<> Nin Situ: Lawrence. <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: What we can do is to end its suffering. Nin Situ: Jason, Lawrence, get ready— Ray Fu: It's pointless.;<> Nin Situ: ...What? Ray Fu: ... <>;<<#Wait(2)>> Ray Fu: You should see this. <<#End>>

title: 進到實驗室裡7
body: //鐵幕緩緩升起 Ray Fu: Marvelous, isn't it? Ray Fu: Our work will usher in a new era of civilization! Ray Fu: This will power the entire world! Thought: There are too many of them! Lawrence Chang: Don't tell me these are all— Thought: So, this is what Vision has been hiding all along... Thought: This is insane... Nin Situ: ... Ray Fu: ... Ray Fu: This is not just a research lab.;<> Ray Fu: Our operation has been going on for a long time. The lab is only a small part of it. We do commercial flop generation on an enormous scale. Ray Fu: Do you know what that insinuates? Ray Fu: You can destroy this particular computing unit. Ray Fu: Or take it out of the lab and give it sort of a life, if you so want. Ray Fu: It's all pointless. Ray Fu: Can you imagine the political implication of our work? Ray Fu: We created a non-human class to exploit, out of thin air! Ray Fu: Lots of people are biased against the flop farming industry because they believe it's inhumane, profiting by exploiting the bottom class. Ray Fu: This is a big step for humanity! <> <<#Wait(1)>> Nin Situ: Do you really think you can keep these things secret forever!? Ray Fu: O-of course not! We are not stupid! Ray Fu: This project will be announced when it's time... Nin Situ: When it's time? Do you really believe the world will ever accept it? Ray Fu: Why wouldn't they? Ray Fu: ... Ray Fu: ... Ray Fu: Ah, I see. Ray Fu: You are the naive one here. Nin Situ: ...What do you mean? Ray Fu: What are these? Just machines, albeit made of flesh, tucked away in an underground lab! Ray Fu: Why would anyone advocate for them, or even care about them? Ray Fu: To the vast majority, they only exist in news stories. Nin Situ: ... <> <<#Wait(1)>> Ray Fu: You do get what I said, right...? Ray Fu: People don't care as much about things that they don't see. Ray Fu: Maybe they don't want to care. That makes life so much easier. Ray Fu: Sweep problems under the carpet and call it a day. Nin Situ: Just pretend it doesn't exist, huh. Ray Fu: Not really... Ray Fu: They put it on the back burner and forget about it. Ray Fu: Can't blame them. Nin Situ: ... <<#End>> //司徒寧把研究員烘出畫面外大力撞牆傳來巨大的聲響

title: 實驗室接露後2
body:
Nin Situ: Change of plan.
Nin Situ: I'm blowing this place up.
Lawrence Chang: What!?
Thought: ...
Nin Situ: This facility is buried deep enough. There won't be much collateral damage even if it caves in.
Lawrence Chang: Wait!
Nin Situ: I must free these manufactured humans from their suffering.
Nin Situ: By law, by common sense, by the minimum decency of humanity, this place must not exist.
Nin Situ: Even with Vision's resources, they can't hide it from the world.
Nin Situ: Now we know for sure that Chameleon was lying. There is no abducted test subjects.
Nin Situ: Then I have no problem killing every living thing in this facility.
Nin Situ: No need for negotiation anymore. All Vision needs is a lethal strike.
Lawrence Chang: You can't just blow it up...
Lawrence Chang: You will die with them.
Nin Situ: I'm unkillable.
Lawrence Chang: You're extra ruthless without 23, Nin.
Nin Situ: ...
Lawrence Chang: Think! Why did Chameleon lie? Obviously he wants you to be here and see this.
Lawrence Chang: Then it has to be trap.
Nin Situ: I agree, but I'm confident enough to beat his trap, whatever it is.
Nin Situ: I have made up my mind.
Lawrence Chang: ...
<<#Simple(8)>>
->What should we do, then?
    Nin Situ: ...
->Kill yourself if you want. Just don't take me with you.
    Nin Situ: You don't need to stay with me.
->...
<<#EndSimple>>
Nin Situ: You two, see to the decryption device.
Nin Situ: We still need all the data we can get, for the final reckoning.
Nin Situ: Then I will start the destruction procedure.
Lawrence Chang: ...
Nin Situ: This place has multiple warehouses for the storage of combustible substances.
Nin Situ: I will deploy explosives in these warehouses, as well as several structurally critical spots.
Nin Situ: Once the alarm system comes back online, I will make some noise to keep them occupied.
Nin Situ: During this time, you will have two options.
Nin Situ: One, pull out as planned, via the shuttle.
Nin Situ: Two, go after Shane Yan while the rest of the mercenaries are busy chasing me.
Thought: Is Yan here...?
Nin Situ: You said you saw the record of Yan entering the premises in the security office, Lawrence?
Lawrence Chang: ...Yes.
Lawrence Chang: But—
Nin Situ: Yan and executives of Vision will know about our intrusion soon enough.
Nin Situ: Then they will flee the country right away.
Nin Situ: Yan must be already on his way out. You have one last opportunity to catch him.
Nin Situ: I should go now.
Nin Situ: Here, take this.;<<PlayUntil(司徒寧,MBU_司徒寧_機動_交付引爆器)>>
Thought: It's a...remote detonator!?
Nin Situ: If you decide to stay, you may need this.
<<#Simple(6)>>
->What do we need it for?
    Nin Situ: For leverage in negotiation, if you get caught.
    Lawrence Chang: Let us go, or else we kill everyone...
    Nin Situ: Exactly.
    Lawrence Chang: And in case you're not able to detonate the charges, we can do it for you.
    Nin Situ: ...
->...
<<#EndSimple>>
Nin Situ: We're running out of time.;<<Set($DLState,司徒寧轉變姿勢準備跑跑)>>
Nin Situ: Think about it while the device works on the data.
Nin Situ: ...;<<Play(張隆生,MBU_安全帽_便服_側站低頭)>>
Nin Situ: See you later.
//司徒寧沖出實驗室外面傳來大量槍生跟戰鬥聲音。
<<#End>>

<<@通用介面首頁>>
Interface: Please select document category.
<<#Simple(99999)>>
->[Public announcements.]
    <<@設施公告首頁>>
    Interface: Select document.
    <<#Simple(99999)>>
    ->[Change of plans.]
        Interface: Hello, all. This is Shane Yan, CEO of Vision Flop Farming.
        Interface: Project Sleeping God is making extraordinary breakthrough. Our technology is now generations ahead of our competitors'.
        Interface: Thank you, everyone, for your hard work.
        Interface: However, after carefully weighing the situation, we believe research and innovation is no longer our top priority.
        Interface: As I said, this is because we are now way too far ahead in the game. Going forward, research labs will see some downscaling of budgets.
        Interface: Please understand that it does not mean Project Sleeping God is over.
        Interface: We are just temporarily slowing down the pace. Keep up the good work.
        Interface: End of message.
        <<#GoTo(@設施公告首頁)>>
    ->[Espionage alert!]
        Interface: As you must have known, there was a leak of confidential information a few months ago, involving Project Sleeping God.
        Interface: We were just informed that one of our competitors is actively working to steal our proprietary technology.
        Interface: Please diligently adhere to all security measures, and report any suspicious personnel.
        Interface: Be assured that there will be appropriate rewards for those who identify and report acts of espionage.
        Interface: End of message.
        <<#GoTo(@設施公告首頁)>>
    ->[Security shift change.]
        Interface: Hi, this is Seth Lo, deputy security director.
        Interface: Be advised that security force at the front entrance will be decreased for today.
        Interface: There is nothing to worry about. Just work as usual with peace of mind.
        Interface: End of message.
        <<#GoTo(@設施公告首頁)>>
    ->[Return]
        <<#GoTo(@通用介面首頁)>>
    <<#EndSimple>>
->[Meeting minutes.]
    <<@會議記錄首頁>>
    Interface: Select document.
    <<#Simple(99999)>>
    ->MM_T02B_Replication of mind
        Interface: As of now, all attempts of embedding replicated minds into computing units have failed. None of them could last over 10 hours.
        Interface: This is most likely the result of pain and terror, which renders the replicated mind unstable.
　　　  Interface: The current makeshift solution is to repeatedly overwrite the mind.
　　　  Interface: Next, the selection of appropriate minds.
　　　  Interface: As we have found out, the diversity of minds contributes to the efficiency of flop generation.
　　　  Interface: For this reason, we must avoid replicating the same mind. Select subjects who have not been replicated before, if possible.
　　　  Interface: End of message.
　　　  <<#GoTo(@會議記錄首頁)>>
    ->MM_R08A_Disposing of flop farms
        Interface: As the Red Room, we maintain our stance on this subject.
        Interface: All outdated flop farms must go.
        Interface: Project Sleeping God, as of now, needs flop farms as the source of replicated minds.
　　　  Interface: But that demand is well served by newer farms.
　　　  Interface: Outdated farms such as Silencio do not offer real value. Their juicers tend to be of dubious quality, too.
　　　  Interface: As such, we call for the accelerated retirement of redundant farms.
　　　  Interface: End of message.
　　　  <<#GoTo(@會議記錄首頁)>>
    ->MM_S01A_Expanding the lossless genetic pool
        Interface: We have only two simple requirements for the hardware of computing units.
    　　Interface: One, a pristine brain.
    　　Interface: Two, a body that supports longer life expectancy.
    　　Interface: Hence we need a rich genetic pool to engineer the ideal hardware for our needs.
    　　Interface: Moreover, all samples in the genetic pool must be lossless, without being excessively cloned/replicated.
    　　Interface: We advise prioritizing younger children, ideally below 5 years of age.
    　　Interface: End of message.
    　　<<#GoTo(@會議記錄首頁)>>
    ->[Return]
        <<#GoTo(@通用介面首頁)>>
    <<#EndSimple>>
->[Personal messages.]
    <<@回到個人通訊>>
    Interface: Select document.
    <<#Simple(99999)>>
    ->[Slow upload.]
        Interface: Mr. Fu, we have noticed that the upload of research data is abnormally slow for the past weeks.
        Interface: Upon preliminary examination, the network itself seems to be alright.
        Interface: The most possible cause is that research data is being uploaded to multiple destinations at the same time.
        Interface: Please check your team for unauthorized use of network, such as keeping unregistered copies or communicating with external sites.
        Interface: End of message.
        <<#GoTo(@回到個人通訊)>>
    ->[We're almost there!]
        Interface: Good news, Ray!
        Interface: I just heard the legislation is about to be passed.
        Interface: If so, they will finally ease the on-premises security clearance.
        Interface: Maybe you'll be allowed to go home soon!
        Interface: End of message.
        <<#GoTo(@回到個人通訊)>>
    ->[Encrypted message] ($DLStateFloat4)
        <<#If($DLStateFloat4=2)>>
            <<#GoTo(@解鎖加密訊息了直接看)>>
        <<#EndIf>>
        Interface: Please enter access code.
        <<Set($DLStateFloat6,0)>>
        <<#Simple(99999)>>
        ->B;<<Set($DLStateFloat6,-1)>>
        ->A
            //
        ->X;<<Set($DLStateFloat6,-1)>>
        ->T;<<Set($DLStateFloat6,-1)>>
        <<#EndSimple>>
        <<#Simple(99999)>>
        ->U
            //
        ->E;<<Set($DLStateFloat6,-1)>>
        ->Q;<<Set($DLStateFloat6,-1)>>
        ->H;<<Set($DLStateFloat6,-1)>>
        <<#EndSimple>>
        <<#Simple(99999)>>
        ->8;<<Set($DLStateFloat6,-1)>>
        ->4;<<Set($DLStateFloat6,-1)>>
        ->0
            //
        ->2;<<Set($DLStateFloat6,-1)>>
        <<#EndSimple>>
        <<#Simple(99999)>>
        ->9;<<Set($DLStateFloat6,-1)>>
        ->6
            //
        ->5;<<Set($DLStateFloat6,-1)>>
        ->1;<<Set($DLStateFloat6,-1)>>
        <<#EndSimple>>
        //IF密碼錯誤
        <<#If($DLStateFloat6=-1)>>
            <<Set($DLStateFloat5,$-1)>>
            Interface: Access code error.
            <<#If($DLStateFloat5=-1)>>
                Interface: You have two more tries.
            <<#EndIf>>
            <<#If($DLStateFloat5=-2)>>
                Interface: You have one more try.
            <<#EndIf>>
            <<#If($DLStateFloat5=-3)>>
                <<Set($DLStateFloat4,-1)>>
                Interface: Excessive retries. Message permanently locked.
            <<#EndIf>>
            <<#GoTo(@回到個人通訊)>>
        <<#EndIf>>
        //if密碼正確
        <<Set($DLStateFloat4,2)>>
        <<@解鎖加密訊息了直接看>>
        Interface: Access code verified. Opening document.
        Interface: Mr. Fu, time is not on our side.
        Interface: Every passing day gives our competitors one extra sliver of chance.
        Interface: I'm sure you still remember the armed assault a few months ago.
        Interface: Now they are trying everything to get hold of what we have on Project Sleeping God.
        Interface: They know we can't mobilize too much security force, lest we attract unwanted attention.
        Interface: For this reason, Mr. Yan has taken unorthodox measures, by hiring mercenary groups.
        Interface: However.
        Interface: The previous internal test of Project Sleeping God was an astonishing success. Our unnamed ally approves.
        Interface: Now that you have implemented connection with their system, codenamed "Cogito"...
        Interface: I suppose the first off-premises demonstration won't be very far away.
        Interface: Of course, the specifics are up to you. You are the professionals on this matter.
        Interface: This message is just a heads up.
        Interface: Lastly, don't you worry about Yan's cut of budgets.
        Interface: He's just a puppet of greater forces, with no understanding about the true value of Project Sleeping God at all.
        Interface: If you're ever short on budget, just let me know. We can provide through separate channels.
        Interface: The shortsighted shall not hinder the course of history.
        Interface: End of message.
        <<#GoTo(@回到個人通訊)>>
    ->[Return]
        <<#GoTo(@通用介面首頁)>>
    <<#EndSimple>>
->[Unplug device.] ($DLStateFloat3)
    <<Set($DLStateFloat3,-1)>>
    <<Set($DLStateFloat2,1)>>
    <<Set($UseFinalTerminal,1)>>
    //播放取下破解器動畫
    Thought: It's done.
    Thought: Don't forget Lawrence's copy.
    <<#GoTo(@通用介面首頁)>>
->[Exit.] ($DLStateFloat2)
<<#EndSimple>>
